# PDF QR Code Scanner

> Client-side web app that scans QR codes from PDF files and exports results as CSV. No backend, no build step, no telemetry.

- `index.html` — single-page UI: file input, scan button, progress bar. Uses Bootstrap 5 for styling.
- `app.js` — UI orchestrator: file selection, calling scanPDF, progress updates, CSV download.
- `scan.js` — deep PDF scanning module. Exports `scanPDF(pdfDoc, options?) → Promise<ScanResult[]>`. Renders PDF pages as offscreen canvases via pdfjs-dist, decodes QR codes via jsQR.
- `scss/custom.css` — compiled Bootstrap 5 CSS.
- Libraries: pdfjs-dist (v4.x, PDF rendering), jsQR (v1.x, QR decoding). Loaded via import map and script tag, served from `node_modules/`.

## Architecture

Single-page, zero-build, ESM module graph. `app.js` is the entry point — it imports `scanPDF` from `scan.js`, both import `pdfjs-dist` via an `<script type="importmap">`. jsQR is loaded as a regular `<script>` that sets `globalThis.jsQR`. No framework, no bundler.

## Scan pipeline

1. User selects PDF → `pdfjsLib.getDocument()` parses it in a web worker.
2. `scanPDF` iterates pages, renders each to an offscreen canvas, runs `jsQR` on pixel data.
3. Results (`{ page, data }[]`) returned to caller.
4. Caller formats as CSV and triggers browser download.

---

https://github.com/tsueri/PDF-QR-Scanner/blob/main/llms.txt
